questions.text: The branch of biology that deals with the organisms living in freshwater bodies is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Environmental Biology is a physical science intersection of environmental science, ecology, evolution, and global change. Environmental biology examines how organisms, species, and communities influence and are impacted by natural and human-altered ecosystems.

Microbiology is the study of microscopic organisms, including viruses, bacteria, algae, fungi, slime moulds, and protozoa. The techniques used to study and manipulate these tiny, mostly unicellular organisms differ from those employed in many other biological fields.
Ecology is the study of living organisms and their interactions with the environment. An ecologist investigates the relationships between these organisms and their habitats.

Freshwater biology is the scientific study of freshwater ecosystems, a branch of limnology. This field seeks to understand the relationships between living organisms and their physical environments, which include rivers, lakes, streams, ponds, and reservoirs.

questions.explanation: Freshwater biology is the branch of biology that deals with the study of organisms living in freshwater bodies such as rivers, lakes, and ponds. It focuses on the interaction of these organisms with their environment.

questions.text: This is a branch of biology that deals with parasites:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Human biology is the study of all aspects of the human organism, including evolution, genetics, ecology, anatomy and physiology, development, anthropology, and nutrition, among others.

Sociobiology is the study of the biological mechanisms that underpin animal and human social behavior. Sociologists have recently incorporated key concepts and explanatory principles from sociobiology.

The study of parasite-host interactions is known as parasitology. Eukaryotic parasites, such as worms, lice, mites, and protozoa, are typically the focus of parasitologists. However, the disciplines of virology, microbiology, and bacteriology are usually where prokaryotic parasites and other infectious agents are studied.

Biotechnology is the application of biological principles to create new products, methods, and organisms that aim to enhance human health and benefit society. It is often referred to as "biotech." This field has been a part of human civilization since its inception, starting with the domestication of plants and animals, as well as the discovery of fermentation.

questions.explanation: Parasitology is the branch of biology that studies parasites, their hosts, and the relationship between them.

questions.text: The members of one species inhabiting the same area are called?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Population, in biology, is defined as all the organisms of the same group or species that live in a specific area and are capable of breeding among themselves. The term “population” encompasses a group of organisms of a single species that can interbreed and live at the same time in the same environment.

Multicellular organisms are those composed of multiple cells. They are classified into 13 major groups of terrestrial living beings, including animals, plants, fungi, ciliates, algae, and foraminifera. The number of cells per organism ranges from some tens to up to several million.

An organ (from the Latin "organum" meaning an instrument or tool) is a collection of tissues that structurally form a functional unit specialised to perform a particular function. Your heart, kidneys, and lungs are examples of organs
A species is often defined as a group of individuals that actually or potentially interbreed in nature. In this sense, a species is the biggest gene pool possible under natural conditions.

questions.explanation: Population, in biology, is defined as all the organisms of the same group or species that live in a specific area and are capable of breeding among themselves. The term “population” encompasses a group of organisms of a single species that can interbreed and live at the same time in the same environment.

questions.text: Which of these is an example of the tissue level in a plant?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Muscle tissue is found in animals, not in plants. It is a type of animal tissue responsible for movement. Muscle tissues are made up of specialized cells (muscle fibers) that can contract and relax to produce motion. There are three main types of muscle tissue in animals — skeletal, smooth, and cardiac — all of which are essential for movement and bodily functions.

Glandular tissue is found in animals, not in plants. It is a type of animal tissue made up of secretory cells that produce and release substances such as enzymes, hormones, or mucus. For example, the salivary glands and gastric glands in humans contain glandular tissue.

The liver is an animal organ, not a part of a plant. The question specifically asks for an example of the tissue level in a plant, so any organ or structure from animals (like the liver) would not fit.

Dermal tissue, for example, is a simple tissue that covers the outer surface of the plant and controls gas exchange. Vascular tissue is an example of a complex tissue and is made of two specialized conducting tissues: xylem and phloem.

questions.explanation: Dermal tissue, for example, is a simple tissue that covers the outer surface of the plant and controls gas exchange. Vascular tissue is an example of a complex tissue and is made of two specialized conducting tissues: xylem and phloem.

questions.text: Which of these is an example of an organ level in plants?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Xylem is a tissue, not an organ. It helps in transporting water and minerals, but since it is only one type of tissue, it doesn’t qualify as an organ.
The stomach is an organ in animals/humans, not in plants. Since the question is about the plant organ level, this is irrelevant.

The stem is an organ in plants. In plants, organs include roots, stems, and leaves. An organ is made up of different tissues (like xylem, phloem, parenchyma, collenchyma, etc.) working together for a common function. The stem helps in support, transport of water and food, and storage.
Mesenchyme is a type of undifferentiated connective tissue in animals (not in plants). So, it is not related to plant organs.

questions.explanation: The stem is an organ in plants. In plants, organs include roots, stems, and leaves. An organ is made up of different tissues (like xylem, phloem, parenchyma, collenchyma, etc.) working together for a common function. The stem helps in support, transport of water and food, and storage.

questions.text: Which organ of a plant has functions of anchoring, storage of food, absorbing water, and minerals:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Transport

options.explanation: The shoot of a plant typically refers to the above-ground portion, including stems, leaves, and reproductive structures such as flowers. While the shoot plays crucial roles in photosynthesis, transportation of water and nutrients, and reproduction, it is not primarily responsible for anchoring the plant or absorbing water and minerals from the soil.

Leaves are primarily responsible for photosynthesis, where they convert sunlight into chemical energy (food) for the plant. They also play a role in gas exchange. While leaves may absorb a minimal amount of water and minerals, they are not specialized for anchoring the plant or serving as the primary site for water and mineral absorption.

Flowers are reproductive structures of a plant and play a vital role in pollination and seed production. They attract pollinators for fertilization. Flowers are not involved in anchoring the plant, storing food, or absorbing water and minerals.

The root is the correct answer because it is the primary organ of a plant that performs the mentioned functions of anchoring, storage of food, and absorbing water, and minerals. Roots anchor the plant firmly in the soil, preventing it from being easily uprooted by wind or other external forces. They store excess carbohydrates, such as sugars and starches, produced during photosynthesis in the leaves. Roots are responsible for absorbing water and minerals from the soil.

questions.explanation: The root anchors the plant in the soil, absorbs water and minerals, and also stores food in some plants like carrots and radishes.

questions.text: A city named Lahore is an example of which level of organization?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: A community refers to all the living organisms in a specific area, but it does not include the non-living components.

A population is a group of organisms of the same species that live together in one area. They share the same environment and resources, and they can reproduce with one another. Because they face similar conditions, their numbers and characteristics can change together over time.
An ecosystem encompasses both the living organisms and the non-living elements in a geographic area, much like a city where humans, animals, plants, buildings, and climate interact.
A species is a group of organisms that can interbreed and produce fertile offspring; it does not include non-living components.

questions.explanation: An ecosystem encompasses both the living organisms and the non-living elements in a geographic area, much like a city where humans, animals, plants, buildings, and climate interact.

questions.text: A large regional community primarily determined by climate is:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Mutualism refers to a type of symbiotic relationship between two different species in which both benefit from the interaction. It does not describe a large regional community primarily determined by climate.

Competition refers to the interaction between organisms where they compete for limited resources such as food, water, or territory. While competition can occur within a biome, it does not define a large regional community solely based on climate.

The correct answer is a biome. A large regional community primarily determined by climate is referred to as a biome. A biome is a specific geographic area characterised by distinct climatic conditions, such as temperature, precipitation, and seasonal variations. The plant and animal species found within a biome are adapted to the prevailing environmental conditions of that region.

Parasitism is a type of symbiotic relationship in which one organism (parasite) benefits at the expense of another organism (host). Parasitism is not specific to a particular climate or region but can occur in various ecosystems.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is a biome. A large regional community primarily determined by climate is referred to as a biome. A biome is a specific geographic area characterized by distinct climatic conditions, such as temperature, precipitation, and seasonal variations. The plant and animal species found within a biome are adapted to the prevailing environmental conditions of that region.

questions.text: The number and variety of species in a place is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: A biome is an area classified according to the species that live in that location. Temperature range, soil type, and the amount of light and water are unique to a particular place and form the niches for specific species allowing scientists to define the biome. However, scientists disagree on how many biomes exist.

The biosphere is made up of the parts of Earth where life exists. The biosphere extends from the deepest root systems of trees to the dark environment of ocean trenches, to lush rainforests and high mountaintops. Scientists describe Earth in terms of spheres.

Biodiversity is all the different kinds of life you'll find in one area—the variety of animals, plants, fungi, and even microorganisms like bacteria that make up our natural world. Each of these species and organisms works together in ecosystems, like an intricate web, to maintain balance and support life.
A phyletic lineage is an unbroken series of species arranged in ancestor-to-descendant sequence, with each later species having evolved from the one which immediately preceded it.

questions.explanation: Biodiversity is all the different kinds of life you'll find in one area—the variety of animals, plants, fungi, and even microorganisms like bacteria that make up our natural world. Each of these species and organisms works together in ecosystems, like an intricate web, to maintain balance and support life.

questions.text: If we accept that birds have wings and that sparrows are birds, then we conclude that sparrows have wings. This is an example of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Inductive reasoning involves drawing general conclusions from specific observations. Here, the reasoning is from a general premise to a specific conclusion.
Deductive reasoning moves from a general statement ('birds have wings') to a specific conclusion ('sparrows have wings').
A hypothesis is a proposed explanation made based on limited evidence, not a form of reasoning used to draw conclusions.
Science is a systematic enterprise that builds and organises knowledge, not a form of logical reasoning.

questions.explanation: Deductive reasoning starts from general statements (all birds have wings) and applies them to specific cases (sparrows) to reach a logical conclusion.

questions.text: Hardy-Weinberg is an example of which entity?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: There is a series of hypotheses supported by the results of many tests, which are then called a theory.
A biological rule or biological law is a generalised law, principle, or rule of thumb formulated to describe patterns observed in living organisms.
Hypothesis is an assumption or concession made for the sake of argument. This is an incorrect option.
Observation is an act or instance of noticing or perceiving. This is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: A biological rule or biological law is a generalized law, principle, or rule of thumb formulated to describe patterns observed in living organisms.

questions.text: Production of genetically identical copies of organisms by asexual reproduction is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Cloning is a technique scientists use to make exact genetic copies of living things. Genes, cells, tissues, and even whole animals can all be cloned. Some clones already exist in nature. Single-celled organisms like bacteria make exact copies of themselves each time they reproduce.

Genetic engineering, also known as genetic modification, is a process that utilises laboratory-based techniques to alter the DNA of an organism. This can involve changing a single base pair (either A-T or C-G), deleting a segment of DNA, or adding a new piece of DNA.
Biological control is the use by humans of beneficial insects such as predators and parasitoids, or pathogens such as fungi and viruses, to control unwanted insects, weeds, or diseases.

Tissue culture (TC) is the cultivation of plant cells, tissues, or organs on specially formulated nutrient media. Under the right conditions, an entire plant can be regenerated from a single cell. Plant tissue culture is a technique that has been around for more than 30 years.

questions.explanation: Cloning is the process of producing genetically identical organisms from a single parent through asexual reproduction.

questions.text: The part of the earth inhabited by living organisms, including land, ocean, and atmosphere:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: The biosphere is made up of the parts of Earth where life exists. The biosphere extends from the deepest root systems of trees to the dark environment of ocean trenches to lush rainforests and high mountaintops. Scientists describe Earth in terms of spheres.

An ecosystem is a geographic area where plants, animals, and other organisms interact with weather and landscape to create a unique environment for life. Ecosystems consist of both biotic factors, which are the living components like plants, animals, and other organisms, and abiotic factors, which are the nonliving elements of the environment.
A social group of any size whose members reside in a specific locality, share government, and often have a common cultural and historical heritage. A locality inhabited by such a group.
A habitat is an environment where an organism lives throughout the year or for shorter periods of time to find a mate. The habitat contains all an animal needs to survive such as food and shelter.

questions.explanation: The biosphere is made up of the parts of Earth where life exists. The biosphere extends from the deepest root systems of trees to the dark environment of ocean trenches to lush rainforests and high mountaintops. Scientists describe Earth in terms of spheres.

questions.text: Which of the following describes coral reefs used by Hakeem in preparing eastern medicines?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Diamond is valued for its beauty and hardness, so it’s mainly used in jewelry and industry rather than healing practices. Traditional medical systems don’t consider it a treatment or remedy. Its role is cultural and ornamental, not medicinal.
Emerald is another precious gemstone and is not typically used in Eastern medicine by Hakeem.
Ruby is a precious gemstone and does not have a significant role in the preparation of Eastern medicines.
Red coral by the name of 'MARJAN' is used by Hakeems in preparing eastern medicines.

questions.explanation: Red coral by the name of 'MARJAN' is used by Hakeems in preparing eastern medicines.

questions.text: Feeding by living in or on the other organism (host) belonging to different species is called?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: This refers to the process by which organisms, such as plants, obtain essential minerals and nutrients from their environment.
Parasites obtain nutrients from their host organism, often causing harm or damage to the host in the process.
Saprophytic nutrition is a type of nutrition where organisms feed on dead and decaying organic matter. They help in the decomposition of organic material.

Symbiotic nutrition involves a mutually beneficial relationship between two different species, where both organisms benefit from the interaction. It's not specifically about one organism living in or on another for nutrition, as in parasitic nutrition.

questions.explanation: Feeding by living in or on another organism (host) belonging to a different species is called **parasitic nutrition** (Option B). Parasites obtain nutrients from their host organism, often causing harm or damage to the host in the process.

questions.text: The transgenic plants, such as golden rice, are produced primarily with the help of:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Escherichia coli is a bacterium that is commonly used in molecular biology research. It is not a natural genetic engineer and cannot transfer its DNA into plant cells.
Proteus mirabilis is a bacterium that causes urinary tract infections. It is not a natural genetic engineer and cannot transfer its DNA into plant cells.

Transgenic plants such as golden rice are produced primarily with the help of Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Agrobacterium tumefaciens is a bacterium that causes crown gall disease in plants. It is also a natural genetic engineer, as it can transfer its own DNA into plant cells. This ability has been exploited to create transgenic plants, which are plants that have had their DNA modified to include genes from other organisms.
Ralstonia solanacearum is a bacterium that causes bacterial wilt in plants. It is not a natural genetic engineer and cannot transfer its DNA into plant cells.

questions.explanation: Transgenic plants such as golden rice are produced primarily with the help of Agrobacterium tumefaciens. Agrobacterium tumefaciens is a bacterium that causes crown gall disease in plants. It is also a natural genetic engineer, as it can transfer its own DNA into plant cells. This ability has been exploited to create transgenic plants, which are plants that have had their DNA modified to include genes from other organisms.

questions.text: Which of the following chemicals is most commonly used to preserve biological specimen?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Formalin is a solution of formaldehyde in water. It is the most common preservative used for biological specimens, as it is effective and inexpensive. Formalin preserves specimens by cross-linking proteins, which prevents them from decaying.

Glutaraldehyde is another aldehyde that is used to preserve biological specimens. It is more expensive than formalin, but it is also more effective at preserving fine details. Glutaraldehyde is often used to preserve specimens for electron microscopy.

Ethylene oxide is a gas that is used to sterilize and preserve biological specimens. It is effective at killing all microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Ethylene oxide is often used to preserve specimens for museums and other collections.

Iodine is a halogen that is used to preserve biological specimens. It is not as effective as the other chemicals on your list, but it is less toxic and can be used to preserve specimens that are intended for human consumption. Iodine is often used to preserve seafood and other foods.

questions.explanation: Formalin is a solution of formaldehyde in water. It is the most common preservative used for biological specimens, as it is effective and inexpensive. Formalin preserves specimens by cross-linking proteins, which prevents them from decaying.

questions.text: All of the following paired organs/structures have homology except:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: A human hand and a bat’s wing share the same basic bone pattern, so they are homologous structures. Both came from a common vertebrate ancestor. They look different now because each adapted to a different function.

The wings of a bat and the wings of a butterfly are analogous structures, but not homologous. Analogous structures are structures that have similar functions but different evolutionary origins. Homologous structures are structures that have similar evolutionary origins but may have different functions. The wings of a bat and the wings of a butterfly are both used for flight, but they have evolved independently from each other. The wings of a bat are modified forelimbs, while the wings of a butterfly are modified outgrowths of the thorax.

options.text: Horse front leg: Front flappers of Whale
options.explanation: The horse’s front leg and the whale’s front flippers are homologous structures. They have the same underlying bone pattern because they came from a common ancestral limb, even though they now serve very different functions.

options.text: Forelimb of a cat: Forelimb of a whale.
options.explanation: They are homologous. They look different and serve different purposes, but the bone layout is the same, showing a shared ancestry.

questions.explanation: The wings of a bat and the wings of a butterfly are analogous structures, but not homologous. Analogous structures are structures that have similar functions but different evolutionary origins. Homologous structures are structures that have similar evolutionary origins but may have different functions. The wings of a bat and the wings of a butterfly are both used for flight, but they have evolved independently from each other. The wings of a bat are modified forelimbs, while the wings of a butterfly are modified outgrowths of the thorax.

questions.text: Identify the correctly matched pair relating to a term and its meaning/ function/ example:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.text: Ecosystem — Organisms of the same species
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because an ecosystem refers to a community of living organisms (plants, animals, and microorganisms) interacting with each other and their physical environment (soil, water, air). Organisms of the same species, however, would be better described by the term population.

options.text: Habitat — The layer of the Earth’s atmosphere
options.explanation: A habitat is the natural environment where an organism lives, including all biotic (living) and abiotic (non-living) factors. The layer of the Earth's atmosphere, particularly the one where weather occurs, is known as the troposphere. Habitat specifically refers to the location or environment of an organism, not to atmospheric layers.

A niche is the role or function of an organism within an ecosystem, including how it gets its energy and nutrients, how it interacts with other organisms, and how it contributes to the energy flow and matter cycling within its ecosystem. The term for a large, distinct ecosystem, particularly one characterized by its climate, flora, and fauna, is biome.

This is the correct match. The biosphere is the part of the Earth that supports life, including the atmosphere, hydrosphere (water), and lithosphere (land). It encompasses all ecosystems and living organisms.

questions.explanation: The biosphere is the part of the Earth that supports life, including the atmosphere, hydrosphere (water), and lithosphere (land). It encompasses all ecosystems and living organisms.

questions.text: The branch of science dealing with the classification of life forms is called:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Taxonomy is the branch of biology that deals with the classification, naming, and identification of organisms. It involves grouping organisms based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

Ecology is the study of the interactions between organisms and their environment. It covers everything from how animals find food to how plants compete for sunlight. In short, it explains how life fits together and how environments shape the organisms living in them.

Morphology focuses on how an organism is built—its shape, form, and physical features. It includes things like body parts, their arrangement, and how these structures help the organism survive. It’s basically the study of what living things look like and how their parts are organized.

Physiology looks at how the parts of living organisms actually work. It covers processes like breathing, circulation, digestion, and how cells use energy. In simple terms, it explains how the body keeps itself alive and functioning.

questions.explanation: Taxonomy is the branch of biology that deals with the classification, naming, and identification of organisms. It involves grouping organisms based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

questions.text: Which of the following can be effective in preventing viral infection in humans?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Antihistamines help ease symptoms like itching, sneezing, and swelling that happen during allergic reactions. They don’t stop viruses or keep someone from getting viral infections, so they aren’t used for prevention or treatment of those illnesses.

Vaccination is a highly effective way to prevent viral infections by:1. Introducing a small, harmless piece of the virus (antigen) to the body2. Stimulating the immune system to produce antibodies and immune cells that recognize and fight the virus3. Providing long-term immunity against specific viruses
Antiseptics can help prevent bacterial infections, but they are not effective against viral infections.
Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections, not viral infections. Using antibiotics against viral infections can even lead to antibiotic resistance.

questions.explanation: Vaccination is a highly effective way to prevent viral infections by:1. Introducing a small, harmless piece of the virus (antigen) to the body2. Stimulating the immune system to produce antibodies and immune cells that recognize and fight the virus3. Providing long-term immunity against specific viruses

questions.text: Vaccination is an example of _:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Vaccination is neither a natural immunity nor a form of passive immunity. They generate active immunity, and it is an example of acquired immunity, so the option is incorrect.
Although vaccination is a type of active immunity, it is not a natural immunity because it is not present from birth. Hence, this option is incorrect.

Vaccines are antigens injected into the body artificially but our body cells are the ones producing antibodies. Hence, it is a type of acquired active immunity. Hence, this option is the correct option.
Although vaccination is an acquired immunity, it is not a passive immunity. It is an example of active immunity, so this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Vaccines are antigens injected into the body (artificially) but our body cells are the ones producing antibodies. Hence, this is an example of acquired active immunity.

questions.text: All of the following are the physical methods to control bacteria except _:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Sterilization is a process that eliminates or destroys all forms of microbial life, including bacteria. It can be achieved through various physical methods such as heat and radiation, which are effective in ensuring complete microbial eradication. Therefore, this option is not the correct answer.

Boiling involves heating a liquid to its boiling point, effectively killing many types of bacteria through thermal action. As a physical method, boiling is widely recognized for its effectiveness in controlling bacterial growth, making this option incorrect.

Radiation uses electromagnetic waves to damage the DNA of microorganisms, preventing their reproduction. This method is considered physical because it does not involve chemical agents. It is commonly utilized in sterilizing food and medical instruments, thus this option is also not the correct answer.

Antiseptics are chemical agents applied to living tissues to reduce the possibility of infection, sepsis, or putrefaction. Unlike the other options, which are physical methods, antiseptics rely on chemical action to control bacteria, making this the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Boiling, radiation, and sterilization are physical methods of controlling bacteria, whereas antiseptics are chemical agents, not physical methods.

questions.text: Which of the following is a connective tissue?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Cartilage is a connective tissue that is composed of cells called chondrocytes and an extracellular matrix that contains collagen and elastin fibers. It is tough but flexible tissue that provides support and cushioning to joints and other structures in the body.
This option is not correct. Epithelium is a tissue that covers the surfaces of organs and forms glands.
This option is also incorrect. Muscle is a tissue that is composed of cells called muscle fibres and is responsible for movement and contraction in the body.
This option is also incorrect. A nerve is a tissue that is composed of cells called neurons and is responsible for transmitting electrical signals throughout the body.

questions.explanation: Cartilage is a connective tissue that is composed of cells called chondrocytes and an extracellular matrix that contains collagen and elastin fibers. It is tough but flexible tissue that provides support and cushioning to joints and other structures in the body. So, option A is correct.

questions.text: BCG vaccine can be used to treat:
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: The BCG (Bacillis Calmette-Guerin) vaccine is primarily used to prevent tuberculosis, a bacterial infection that primarily affects the lungs.
The BCG vaccine is not effective against typhoid, a bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi that can cause fever, abdominal pain, and other symptoms.
The BCG vaccine is not effective against cholera, a bacterial infection caused by Vibrio cholerae that can cause severe diarrhea and dehydration.
The BCG vaccine is also not effective against pneumonia, which can be caused by various bacteria, viruses, and other pathogens.

questions.explanation: The BCG (Bacillis Calmette-Guerin) vaccine is primarily used to prevent tuberculosis, a bacterial infection that primarily affects the lungs. So, option A is encircled.

questions.text: Which of the following is not an example of disuse of organs?
questions.subject: Biology
questions.topic: Biology and its Major Fields of Specialization

options.explanation: Snakes are an example of disuse of organs because they have evolved to lose their legs over time, as they are no longer necessary for their lifestyle.
Shedding of milk teeth is a natural process that occurs as part of normal development, not a result of disuse of organs.
Muscle atrophy can also occur as a result of disuse of organs, such as when a limb is immobilized for a prolonged period.

The movement of the ear is an example of an vestigial organ, meaning it is a remanent of an organ that had a function in ancestors of the species, but has since lost that function. The ear muscles may still be capable of movement, but are no longer necessary for hearing in humans.

questions.explanation: Shedding of milk teeth is a natural process that occurs as part of normal development, not a result of disuse of organs.
